1771501038.name: Falafel King
1771501038.review: Pretty pretty good I think. In petticoat lane market, only open weekdays. £5.50 for the Whole Shabang, which has falafel, haloumi, and auburgine. Delightful. Five stars.

1771501554.name: Alexandria Cafe
1771501554.review: Lovely place, family run, etc etc. Great Egyptian food. They have falafel stuffed with cheese, which I did not have but which I would like to have. My wrap was very good and when I finished it I wished that I had a bigger stomach, such that I could have had another. Nice place to sit and read your book as well. Five stars.

1771508463.name: Nesma Falafel
1771508463.review: Lovely place, lovely man. The sauce was a little salty but the falafel itself was incredible. So fresh and the inside is so green. Nice music too. Five stars.

1771508487.name: Purely Falafel
1771508487.review: Meant for UCL students, only open weekdays. Sometimes you get a falafel while you wait. I like to run here in the morning and get a wrap, then run home with it and have it for lunch. Very hunter-gatherer of me. Five stars.

1771508510.name: Ali Baba Kebab House London
1771508510.review: I went here one night and an old man tried to pay for my falafel wrap. This was ~2 min after he watched me order and pay for it myself. Maybe he just forgot? He was guessing the ages of everyone that came in and he was always about 10 years too young, which people liked. The falafel was pretty good. Five stars.

1771508551.name: Round falafel
1771508551.review: A round hole in the wall. Their menu is bigger than the place itself, but it just lists different combinations of things you can put in your wrap. I.e. falafel and hummus, falafal and auburgine, falafel and haloumi, falafel and hummus and haloumi, chicken and falafel, falafel and cauliflower, etc. I always get falafel and auburgine. Discount if you pay cash. Five stars.

1772539050.name: Woody’s Camden
1772539050.review: They are open until 3am. Five stars.

1772539110.name: Burger Inn Café Kebab
1772539110.review: I would not call it fine cuisine. The falafel was microwaved. The wrap was mostly shredded carrots, for some reason. But I was hungry and it was a big portion, and also the name of this place is the Burger Inn Café & Restaurant Kebab, which is pretty great. Five stars.

1772539138.name: Fal Falafel
1772539138.review: Hard to eat. Unknowable hours. The pigeons and gulls will watch you intently, jealously, covetously. Five stars.
1772539138.address: The Moor, Post Office, The Moor, Falmouth

1772539154.name: FalafelFellas
1772539154.review: Good Falafel, good fellas. A cart outside the station, open every day but Sunday. It's cheap, considering the area. And they have an NHS discount. Five stars.

1772539172.name: Eye Falafel
1772539172.review: Perhaps the best, if you can stomach the line. Weekdays, Petticoat Lane Market. Maybe go on a friday at 1pm. Avoid the lunch rush. But incredible falafel, very fluffy, flavorful, and you get a whole lot of it. None of that '3 falafel in a wrap' tomfoolery here. Your wrap is full to the brim. Splendid stuff. Five stars.
1772539172.address: Brunswick House, 14 New Goulston St, London

1772539198.name: Baba Ganoush
1772539198.review: You can get an extra spicy falafel wrap here. Five stars.

1772539217.name: Hoxton Beach Whitecross Street
1772539217.review: You can buy falafel in bulk, £5.50 for 10 pieces. Five stars.

1772539234.name: Falafel Street food
1772539234.review: This place is across from the Photo Book Cafe, which is pretty cool. Five stars.

1772539283.name: Ridley Rd
1772539283.review: There's a little cart here that makes a good wrap. Really oily falafel (not in a bad way), crunchy but super soft inside. Five stars.

1772539311.name: Mersin Tantuni Dalston Restaurant
1772539311.review: Wow! They have a wrap called Faloumi. It's falafel and halloumi. Pretty neat. Five stars.

1772539333.name: Istanbul Restaurant
1772539333.review:
When in Derby, eat a falafel wrap I guess.

There are a bunch of great greek restaurants in Derby, so maybe try those first, but the falafel wrap here was ok too. Five stars.

1772539358.name: Falafel Zaki Zaki
1772539358.review: This is where I would go for lunch if I was a member of parliament. Five stars.

1772539379.name: Hiba Street Food
1772539379.review: There are definitely better wraps in the area, but this is the only one that's open on weekends. Five stars.

1772539394.name: Cafe Metro Express
1772539394.review: Lovely place. Only open on weekdays. You can get a little pastry too. Five stars.

1772539414.name: Mr Falafel
1772539414.review: I ran 10 miles to get this wrap. It wasn't 10 miles away from where I started, but I got lost. It was all worth it in the end. I'm jealous of Mrs Falafel. Five stars.
1772539414.address: New Shepherds Bush Market, Units T4/T5, Uxbridge Rd, London

1772539429.name: Basta - Falafel & Grill
1772539429.review: Basta! Basta! Just kidding, I could eat a little more. Five stars.
1772539429.address: Unit 708, Camden Stables Market, 45 Chalk Farm Rd, London

1772539450.name: Falafel Shack
1772539450.review: Sometimes, they'll sell you a wrap for £3. Nothing is that cheap. Seems unethical. Five stars.
1772539450.address: The Merchant Building, 41a Goulston St, London

1772539465.name: ShakeShuka
1772539465.review: Welcome to Shakeshuka (We don't take bookings for up to 4 guests). Five stars.

1772539481.name: Falafel Guys ‐ Northern Market
1772539481.review: This is where I would go for lunch with my coworkers if I was a millennial working a nondescript mildly creative white collar office job in Leeds. Also they have three (!) kinds of hummus. Five stars.

1772539497.name: Akkawi
1772539497.review: You can order and wait in the shade on the other side of the street to get out of the sun and then when you get your wrap you can walk to try and find somewhere green and nice to sit and eat it and you wander for ages trying to stay out of the sun but the sun has a way of finding your side of the street and hissing down at your neck and your nose and the tips of your ears and you just want to eat your falafel wrap but there is no shade in this horrible town and you carry your wrap for miles and miles searching for a little out-of-the-way tree with over-the-head leaves but there's not a tree in sight in this desert of old industry and empty sky and beating sun and too-naked northerners and you round a corner and go over the tracks and there! there! a tree! and you sit and eat your falafel wrap. Five stars.
1772539497.address: Trinity Shopping Centre, 56 Albion St, Leeds

1772539515.name: Hoxton Beach Goodge Place
1772539515.review: This is a chain. There's another one of these on this map. Can you find it? Ooh, homework. Five stars.

1772539544.name: Nosh
1772539544.review: There's a falafel wrap on the menu. It's like a sandwich, square, but wrapped in a tortilla and cut diagonally. Structural integrity is a weak point. A gymnastic experience to eat without it falling apart. This is where you go when your coworkers want a lamb wrap and you say ok I'll come with. Five stars.

1772539571.name: Mrs Falafel
1772539571.review: Mrs Falafel is a big yellow box next to a petrol station. Mrs Falafel is open until 3am. They say god is a woman - maybe this is her? Five stars.

1772539587.name: شاورما القمة The top shawarma
1772539587.review: Well give me falafel and spin me round, because this place is the top. Five stars.

1772539602.name: Fern Coffee Bar
1772539602.review: I wasn't expecting there to be falafel wraps here and I wasn't in the mood for one exactly but it's all they had and I needed something to sink my little teeth into. Five stars.

1772539633.name: Pasha Glasgow
1772539633.review: Wow! I am pasha nate about this place. Five stars.

1772539688.name: King of Falafel
1772539688.review:
Now that I am Mr Big Shot with a Falafel Wrap Map I feel the need to go to new falafel places (King of Falafel) even though tried and tested falafel places (Woody's) might be right across the street. I suppose it is good to broaden one's horizons. Five stars.

Edit: With a broader sample size and higher quality data (eating falafel while sober?), I think this place is better than the one across the street. The aubergine's a treat. Five more stars.

1772539740.name: Joy Cafe
1772539740.review: I found myself here, falafel wrap in hand, at the end of a 10 mile run. The wrap was dense as a brick but it did, as promised, bring me joy. Five stars.

1772539761.name: YEMANES'
1772539761.review:
Peckham is pretty much on the other side of the planet, but now you find yourself here, far from all that you know, weary from days, weeks, months of travel, unsure if you'll ever see home again. You've crossed the mighty Thames, weathered great storms, and survived dysentery. How long have you been travelling? You've been underground and overground, national rail and rail replacement, little lime bike and big red bus. There are wrinkles on your face you didn't have when you left home, bright eyed, dreaming of adventure. You've grown old. Will you ever make it home? You have a son; remember him? When you see him—if you ever see him again—will you still recognise him?

In any case, you could probably do with some falafel, and there's no better place than YEMANES' to raise your spirits for the long journey back to North London. Five stars.

1772539783.name: E. Mono Restaurant Kentish Town
1772539783.review: It was a hefty wrap. Like a baby, swaddled in parchment and soaked in tahini. Very not bad. Five stars.

1772539798.name: Kamil Bakery
1772539798.review: Warehouse falafel. Industrial estate falafel. Big white van falafel. Good falafel. Five stars.

1772539813.name: Time Art Café
1772539813.review: The funny thing about cafe art is that it's all so different, and yet all so the same. I don't really mean that as a compliment. Five stars.

1772539834.name: Efes Restaurant & Bar
1772539834.review: Catch a glimpse of chef Kazim; have a wrap of falafel. It looks like a sit-down situation but they have a takeaway counter and you can take your wrap and fly, fly, fly 'cross the cross-streets towards lunch in the park. Five stars.

1772539925.name: pockets
1772539925.review: Here's a psychological phenomenon: the longer you wait in a line, the less likely you are to say anything bad about the thing you waited in line for, lest you look like a bandwagonning fool. Five stars.

1772539943.name: Fabulous Falafel
1772539943.review: I'm pretty sure there was cholula in my falafel wrap. Five stars?

1772539985.name: Ttin wa zeytoon Ltd
1772539985.review: You know, it was a pretty good wrap. And I like the name of this place. Five stars.

1772540001.name: Falafel Sara
1772540001.review: I walked past this place today and realized that 1) I ate here sometime in the distant past and 2) it was not on my falafel map. Five stars.

1772540021.name: Cosmic Kitchen
1772540021.review: Find far-out falafel, beyond the beetroot belt, where spears of pickled turnips pierce the kaleidoscopic night, auroras of aubergine purple and salad green, red-onion rings around celestial falafel, planetary falafel, gravitational falafel, falafel so big they don't fit in the terrestrial sky, falafel twisting, twirling, spinning through the black vast reaches of space. Five stars.

1772540035.name: Pilpel Fenchurch Street
1772540035.review: Falafel in a pocket: eat it for lunch. This is silver spoon falafel. Golden platter falafel. Business class falafel. Sky garden falafel. Leisurely lunch break falafel. The kind of falafel you can only eat if you can afford to spend hours nibbling away, gymnastic, acrobatic, making sure no leaf or ancient grain of any of the seven different salads in the pita drops to the floor. It's not like the cheap, crude falafel wraps of the hoi polloi. Treat yourself, I guess. Five stars.
1772540035.address: Unit 9, 30 Fenchurch Street, 11 Rood Ln, London

1772540058.name: Zappa's
1772540058.review: Go to Zappa's! Eat a falafel wrap, the miracle of the microwave! Relish it! Eat it on the banks of the Dart! Five stars.

1772540076.name: Falafel
1772540076.review: An end of the line wrap, a terminus wrap, a north of the northern line wrap, an edge of the tube map wrap. Eat it on the train, barreling southwards into the heat-dome city, screeching into the tunnels, crunching into falafel. Five stars.

1772540091.name: Maoz
1772540091.review: The bread they use for their wraps is very thin and very strong. Good tensile strength. Flexural strength is also great, but that's kind of a given. When you're running with a falafel wrap, material science is very important. A falafel wrap carried baton-like through central London undergoes various stresses, all sorts of bumps, jumps, zigs and zags. Structural integrity matters a lot, and on structural integrity, Maoz delivers. Five stars.

1772540108.name: Alushi
1772540108.review: Wow! Falafel in a little cave. They have all sorts of other fun wraps, but when you have a falafel map, you feel compelled to get falafel. Next time I will get something else. Exciting! Five stars.
1772540108.address: Brighton Railway Station, 1 Trafalgar Arches, Brighton and Hove, Brighton

1772540125.name: Falafel house Lebanese
1772540125.review: Wow! Falafel as pillowy as the clouds above. I would get the best sleep of my life on a bed of this falafel. Five stars.

1772540141.name: AMI BAGDAD
1772540141.review: Très délicieux, ce wrap du falafel. Cinq étoiles.

1772540159.name: FiL FiL Mediterranean Grill
1772540159.review: I call it a success, this first falafel foray into international wraps. Fil fil up my belly with American falafel, I guess. My map's gone multinational, and anyway aren't we all just living on a big ball of falafel spinning cosmic around the sun? What a world: garbonzo asteroids, red-onion rings around a green-olive moon. A dash of milky tahini across the infinite sky. Call it a constellation: five stars.

1772540177.name: Semoorg Falafel
1772540177.review: This is a good place to go when you run all the way to far Wimbledon with the hope of falafel firm in your mind but when you arrive you find the stand of your fantasies closed, shuttered, dusty and weather-worn. There is no falafel to be had in Wimbledon this day. But do not dispair! For the train you take to get home brings you to Waterloo station, and Waterloo station means Semoorg Falafel. All is well. Five stars.

1772540192.name: Warren Street Falafel
1772540192.review: There'll be Warren on the Street when they run out of falafel, I'll tell you that much. Five stars.

1772540206.name: Go Falafel - Queen Victoria Street
1772540206.review: Go Falafel, but maybe go away from the wrap and get the bowl. There was nothing despicable to me about this falafel, but like Gru's trousers, the wrap is too small and it squeezes everything out of the top and onto your own trousers. Five stars.

1772540263.name: TCR Falafel
1772540263.review: TCR might stand for Totally sCRumptious, but, after looking at the prices, it could also stand for That's CRazy. If I didn't have a map for falafel I never would have taken out the mortgage required to eat here. But what did I expect? It's a permanent stall on Tottenham Court Road. Five years of loan repayments? Five stars.
1772540263.address: Kiosk Outside, 7 Tottenham Ct Rd, London

1772540305.name: Falafel restaurant
1772540305.review:
Go here on your first day, before your friends arrive, because of a review you read on The Street Food Guy Dot Com. Find the neon sign that says "Falafel", and ask for some. It's nothing special, but, as you're leaving, you spot something down the street. It's another falafel restaurant! And it looks a lot more like the pictures you saw on The Street Food Guy Dot Com. Only problem? You're already full!

Come back on the last day, and bring your friends. Tell them about The Street Food Guy Dot Com, and tell them how excited you are to try the real best falafel in Marrakesh, not the falafel next to the real best falafel in Marrakesh. Order your falafel, salivate in expectation, take a bite, and realize it's the exact same falafel that you had four days ago. It's the same restaurant. Oh well. Five stars.

1772540380.name: Cafe Falafel
1772540380.review:
Here you can sit and watch the cats and order falafel, even though there are more interesting things on the menu, because you have a Wrap Map, and your Wrap Map is hungry for more.

It's not great falafel, but it's a great place to sit. Five stars.

1772540403.name: Hiba Taboun
1772540403.review: Ride your bicycle north, east, range far beyond your usual tramping grounds. Enter the forest, lose yourself in the trees and spin your wheels in the mud. Cold, hungry, crawl on two wheels to Walthamstow, where falafel awaits. Cavernous stomach, hungry eyes. Good falafel. This is why you own a bicycle. Five stars.

1772540419.name: Beirut Kitchen Street
1772540419.review: You can choose between adding aubergine or cauliflower to your falafel wrap, which starts a war in the mind so large it's hard to remember which way is up anymore. You can tumble in your indecision for hours, as happy people come and go with their happy wraps, or you can do what I did, which was to choose aubergine, because it was listed first on the chalkboard sign. I'm sure the cauliflower would have been good too, but the aubergine was really good. Yum yum! It's a tasty life, having an abundance mindset. Five stars.

1772540459.name: MEZZA EXPRESS
1772540459.review:
This is the taste of despair. You came for a camera - all this way for a camera - and you didn't even find it. They sold it before you arrived. You are spit back onto the street, and as the thick door of the camera shop slams behind you, it starts to rain. You are the image of desperation. You are a fool. Why did you come here? Why did you think that things would work out? You walk the garish streets, rain on your nose, rain on your glasses, rain down the back of your neck. The cars kick puddles onto your boots. You try to find shelter, but there is none, and so you wander. You walk along the river, watch the rain pat down the rippling eddies, glance into back gardens where patio furniture sits, empty, dripping. and into the windows of home offices, where the backs of computer monitors obscure all but the tops of heads and typing fingers. Your trousers are soaked, and you can barely see through your glasses, and you find yourself, all of the sudden, grabbing the handle of a door, and pulling, and then you are inside, and you wipe your eyes and ask for a falafel wrap with halloumi.

Despair! You are hungry, concavely, but the train is sooner, and you run off, falafel in hand, towards the station. On the train, you stand in the door, too self-conscious to eat your wrap. You'll have it when you get to Waterloo, you think, but half an hour later you are off the train, running down to the Northern Line, and then you are home, and your wrap is hours old, and why on Earth haven't you eaten it yet? And you take off your wet trousers and hang them over your bed frame and, shivering in cold and hollow hunger, you sit down and eat your falafel. This is the taste of salvation. Five stars.

1772540485.name: Holy Land Grocery & Bakery
1772540485.review: Take a friend. Make him drive. Order a falafel wrap for yourself, because of this map, a falafel plate for him, because he can't eat gluten, and a side of tabbouleh, because you like to have fun sometimes. The day is cold; ask for tea. When your food is ready, and you're done ogling the trays of homemade baklava, the jars of beans, the boxes and boxes of scoop-your-own tea-by-the-pound, leave that palace of incandescence. Drive around the corner, and sit on the bleachers in Kilmer Park, shivering, watching the empty baseball field like you're two male leads in a coming-of-age film, passing the tea back and forth to warm your fingers. Open the bag: there's a paper bundle, for you, and a plastic container, for him. As it turns out, the falafel plate is just a falafel wrap with fries, so eat your wrap and watch your friend pick the falafel out of the bread with the spork you keep in your backpack. When he's done, you can use the strips of his leftover pita as tabbouleh spoons. Your fingers are too cold, and you drop tabbouleh on the ground, but most of it makes it into your mouth. Five stars.

1772540499.name: Toucan Food & Custard
1772540499.review: It is like coming out of the desert. It is like seeing land, after so many days at sea. The electric sign blinks. You're driving past, read Toucan Food & Custard, and then it blinks again, and you read Falafel & Shawarma. Falafel? Here? Falafel, at a custard stand in Port Washington, Wisconsin, USA? Salvation arrives in unlikely forms. You screech to a halt, the car whips around. Inside, you are giddy. You watch the tv behind the counter, a carousel of pictures of everything on offer. They have sloppy joes. They have gyro fries. They have ice cream sundaes, baba ghanoush, mini corn dogs, 15 kinds of banana splits, a Lake Perch dinner, a chicken fajita wrap combo, a pizza burger, and, of course, falafel. You can hardly contain yourself. You order and sit and watch the tv. And then you are the proud new holder of a falafel wrap. It is warm. It is wonderful. It's probably as good as you're going to get, here in Wisconsin, USA. Five stars.

1772540516.name: Aladdin
1772540516.review: They can't all be winners. Maybe London and Milwaukee are apples and oranges. Maybe this is just what a falafel wrap is like in the cold Midwest. Maybe there's no such thing as good and bad. Maybe it's just a matter of accepting our differences. Five stars.
1772540516.address: Milwaukee Public Market, 400 N Water St, Milwaukee

1772540534.name: YALLA FALAFEL
1772540534.review: It's a little far from where you're staying, but it's worth the walk, even more so than other falafel (which is always worth a walk). The falafel is warm and soft and basically jumps into your mouth. You can get a falafel wrap and loose falafel in a beautiful little bowl. You can get a bowl of hummus, maybe the best hummus you've ever had, with whole cashews and little lake of melted butter in the middle. Why hadn't you thought to pour melted butter over a bowl of hummus? This is why Icarus fell from the sky, maybe: too much of a good thing. Five stars.
1772540534.address: Cihannüma Mahallesi. Serencebey Yokuşu, Fırat iş merkezi, No: 1/B, Beşiktaş

1772540577.name: Falafella (falafel)
1772540577.review: This is a good place to go after a morning run, and also after a night out. I never saw the door not open. It's a kind of salvation, slices of aubergine this big, like the moon in the sky come to bring you back to life. The Falafella Specific wrap is the most exciting. Definitely specific. I don't know what was in it, but there was nothing I would have added to it, and nothing I would have taken away. Kind of the perfect experience. Five stars.

1772540598.name: Nohut Falafel & Humus (Glutensiz Vegan Vejetaryen)
1772540598.review: Ok, look. I didn't actually have a falafel wrap here. I was pulled astray by the siren song of the falafel plate, which had a lot going on. All sorts of hummus, something yogurty. A falafel spa, many different sauces and spreads, all kinds of places for a falafel to take a dip. I imagine it most refreshing. Yum! Five stars.

1772540620.name: Naar Lebanese takeaway
1772540620.review: Good sauce? Unsure. Savior on a Tuesday night when all the other restaurants are closed. Five stars.

1772540649.name: Falafel Bar Finchley
1772540649.review: Another round! Put it on my tab at the old falafel bar. Yum yum. Five stars.

1772540666.name: Falafel & Shawarma London
1772540666.review: It's not a good idea to get your hopes up, unless you're getting your hopes up about Falafel & Shawarma London. It took me a full year to make it this far south after it was recommended to me, and it was a full year of unbridled excitement, a full year of ceaseless anticipation, which is the best sauce. I was not disappointed. Get yours with aubergine. Five stars.

1772540684.name: Plantkind
1772540684.review: Great falafel in Shrewsbury? It’s probably about as common as you think (not very), but this place is rare. Rarified. This is a place of lip-lick falafel, sock-off falafel, revelation falafel. A falafel wrap good enough to blow the mind of a brass gourmet. A falafel wrap holy enough to make a pirate praise the lord. A falafel wrap strong enough to blow a brigantine all the way to Tonga. This falafel really sets the soul on fire. Really wiggles the wheels. Really whacks the mole. Really punches the monkey. Falafel wrap so good I’m making up phrases. Five stars.

1773271355.name: Falafel Highbury
1773271355.review: Bog-standard falafel. Regulation falafel. ISO falafel. Nothing special about this falafel, but when you have a wrap map, you have to say something. The most unusual part of this falafel wrap was that the paper stuck to the bread, and I ended up eating some of the paper. Not in a bad way. I kind of liked it? Five stars.

1774799779.name: Restaurante Jerusalén
1774799779.review: Wow! Salvation falafel for the Semana Santa! I haven’t had falafel this good in a long time. A cross-cultural miracle! And God said: let there be falafel. No more patatas bravas for me. Call it Amazing Grace: I was hungry / but now I’m full. Five stars.

1775062334.name: EL CAMPESINO
1775062334.review: I'm not sure how to reconcile this encounter with my previous falafel experiences. It wasn't like any wrap I've had before. The hummus made it creamy, like an egg salad sandwich, and the pita folded funny, half-circular, like a pasty. The falafel was in a bowl in the deli counter and my hopes were not high but they were certainly exceeded! A new experience. Five stars.

1775232315.name: Yafa Restaurante
1775232315.review: ¡Guao! ¡Bien sabroso! Qué magnifico este falafel. Cinco estrellas.

1775463214.name: Falafel Damasco El Rey del Falafel
1775463214.review: I’d been in Spain for a week when I had this falafel wrap, and it was the first spicy thing I’d found. Why is it so hard for a little guy to get a little kick in the taste buds? Five stars.

1775933086.name: Olives and Meze Soho
1775933086.review: I have bought enough falafel wraps in my life (and am privileged to have done so) that I think £9.99 is an absurd amount of money. Apparently, £9.99 is the deal price. They’re not normally this cheap. That being said, the falafel was a-ok and the fillings were fresh and flavourful and generally pleasant upon the tongue. And anyway, £9.99 for a full meal in the center of the center of London isn’t too bad, I guess. They probably pay a lot for rent. Five stars.

1776191722.name: Viva Falafel
1776191722.review: Tabbouleh? In a falafel wrap? I should expect this kind of luxury, on Oxford street. Call it a falafel hotel: five stars.

1776948149.name: L'As du Fallafel
1776948149.review:
This place has a reputation! If you mention you're going to Paris, chances are slim you'll get recommended the Louvre, or Notre-Dame, or the Eiffel Tower. In my experience, ninety-nine times out of a hundred, you'll be told to go to L'As du Fallafel. Apparently, the lines are long. Apparently, the falafel is incredible. Apparently, it's a cultural icon.

I wish the line was longer. I wish I had to struggle more for my falafel. But the line was quick, and the people were friendly, and the falafel was unoffensive. At L'As du Falafel, I reached neither the highs nor the lows of human experience. Some things are only gritty on the outside. But now I can say that I've been. Now I can say that I've taken a bite out of the middle of the France-Falafel venn diagram. Five stars.

1777222657.name: Marché alimentaire Bastille
1777222657.review: There is a stall in this market with a sign that says “falafel”, because that is something they sell. I tried it. I am glad I did, because it reminded me that not all falafel is incredible. It reminded me to be grateful when good falafel comes my way. It was thankfulness falafel. Perspective falafel. Five stars.

1777226412.name: Chez Hanna
1777226412.review: Delightfully small falafel! Little marbles all clumped together like protons and neutrons - nothing’s the Matter with these little guys. Yum yum! Five stars.

1778086066.name: Hawa Beirut
1778086066.review: There is a concept in architecture called compression and release. The idea is that a small, tight, claustrophobic space, like a narrow hallway or a low-ceilinged foyer, will make a larger room feel even bigger, grander, even more expansive. This is the role of Hawa Beirut. A small, tight, frankly disappointing falafel experience in the morning is perfect for making the afternoon's falafel, a few blocks away, feel all the more magical. Five stars.

1778086347.name: Le daily syrien veggie
1778086347.review: Last-ditch falafel, my final chance before the Eurostar, and boy howdy did it deliver. On my last day in Paris, I found great falafel. Très bien. Five stars.

1781781485.name: Liban Bites - Lebanese Grill
1781781485.review: Consolation falafel, when you’re down on your luck, when you’re feeling like you could have done more. When you just missed your train, forced to wait for the bus, try not think about how the falafel you’re eating is the reason you missed it. Consolation falafel costs £9 and all the time spent waiting for a bus that won’t come, all the while thinking that you could be in bed. This is falafel for to wipe away tears: five stars.

1781782019.name: Baba Ganoush Kitchen - Jerusalem Falafel
1781782019.review: World’s best falafel? They might be right. The chef is deaf and the dog has a gall stone and the wrap was as long as my arm (almost). The guy on the radio was talking about class warfare and everything smelled so good and I carried my wrap like I may one day carry my baby to the nearby park, to the shady, dew-licked grass, and I let out a sigh and wiggled my toes and commenced the gymnastic endeavour of eating it. Five stars.
1781782019.address: 81 St Nicholas' Rd, St Paul's, Bristol
